concept_ref: §4 Level 4 — 'Four-conditional vault alarm. BCC requires 16 tests; MCDC requires 5.'

scenario.title: FINAL BOSS — Vault Authorization
scenario.narrative: Access to a high-security vault requires a valid badge AND a non-expired PIN AND the request to fall within the time window AND a positive biometric scan. BCC would need 2^4 = 16 tests; certify the vault with the MCDC minimum of 5. Pick exactly 5 rows from the 16-row truth table that satisfy MCDC for ALL FOUR conditions.
scenario.code:
if (badge_valid and not pin_expired and time_window_ok and biometric_ok):
    grant_access()

claim: Choose exactly 5 of the 16 truth-table rows so that each of A, B, C, D has a valid MCDC independence pair.
charges: Vault access is granted only when the badge is valid AND the PIN is not expired AND the request falls within the time window AND the biometric scan is positive.
The court certifies the vault under MC/DC coverage of the four-condition predicate `badge_valid && !pin_expired && time_window_ok && biometric_ok`.

The prosecution alleges that any test selection other than the canonical 5-row N+1 pattern (one all-true baseline plus one row per condition flipped to false) leaves at least one condition without a valid independence pair.

options.label: R1  (T,T,T,T → grant)
options.explanation: Baseline. The shared 'all true' row that pairs with each of R2-R5 to form an independence pair.

options.label: R2  (F,T,T,T → deny)
options.explanation: Pairs with R1: only A flips, outcome flips. Independence pair for A.

options.label: R3  (T,F,T,T → deny)
options.explanation: Pairs with R1: only B flips, outcome flips. Independence pair for B.

options.label: R4  (T,T,F,T → deny)
options.explanation: Pairs with R1: only C flips, outcome flips. Independence pair for C.

options.label: R5  (T,T,T,F → deny)
options.explanation: Pairs with R1: only D flips, outcome flips. Independence pair for D.

options.label: R6  (F,F,T,T → deny)
options.explanation: Trap. R6 differs from R1 in TWO conditions (A and B), so it cannot be an independence pair for either alone.

options.label: R12 (F,F,F,T → deny)
options.explanation: Trap. Three conditions differ from R1 — not a valid pair for any single condition.

options.label: R16 (F,F,F,F → deny)
options.explanation: Trap. All four conditions differ from R1. Useful for BCC, useless for MCDC.

correct_answer_explanation: Pick {R1, R2, R3, R4, R5}. Each of R2-R5 differs from R1 in exactly one condition AND the outcome flips — that's the canonical N+1 = 5 MCDC pattern from Annex C.2.3.6.
wrong_answer_explanation: MCDC needs, for each condition, a pair of tests where ONLY that condition flips and the outcome flips. With AND-chains, the outcome only flips between TTTT and a one-condition-false row. Anchor on R1 and add R2..R5.
hints: MCDC needs, for each of A, B, C, D, two tests where ONLY that condition flips and the overall outcome flips. With AND-chains, the outcome flips only between TTTT and one-condition-false. Re-read Law Card 6.
Anchor on R1=(T,T,T,T) — the only TRUE outcome. The independence partner for each condition is the row where that single condition is flipped to F: R2 for A, R3 for B, R4 for C, R5 for D.

Solution — Pick {R1, R2, R3, R4, R5}. Each pair shares R1 as the baseline, flips exactly one condition, and flips the outcome from grant → deny. That's MCDC in 5 tests instead of BCC's 16. This is the canonical N+1 pattern from Annex C.2.3.6 and the cost-saving that DO-178C exploits.

seeded_faults.description: Biometric check D is silently bypassed when the badge is valid — the bug is invisible without an MCDC pair on D.

misconceptions.explanation_md: The 5-test MCDC solution is the canonical N+1 pattern: one shared 'all true' baseline plus one row per condition where that condition alone flips to false. The temptation is to add 'isolation' rows like R6=(F,F,T,T) — but these flip TWO conditions, which is not a valid independence pair for either. MCDC's power is precisely this discipline: each pair must change exactly one condition while the others stay fixed.
